title: tetracera costata subsp. rotundifolia
intro: Tetracera costata subsp. rotundifolia, also known as the East Indian siris or round-leaved Tetracera, is a plant species native to Southeast Asia. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly as a treatment for fever, cough, and respiratory issues due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Tetracera costata subsp. rotundifolia include its ability to relieve bronchitis, asthma, and other respiratory problems, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
